text: Legitimacy primitive: human approval/veto checkpoint for consequential autonomous actions, with an audit trail
description: The "human stays final authority" primitive from the GTM thesis (7032fa7f). Before the autonomous loop takes consequential or hard-to-reverse actions (real-world actions, spending, external comms, destructive ops), surface them for human approval/override with a clear, auditable record of what was proposed and decided. Builds on the existing watchdog approval surface (WatchdogActionsOverlay.vue) but generalizes it from 'approve a terminal action' to 'approve a consequential real-world/irreversible action', and ties decisions to the aim they serve. Tonight's over-cautious push-gating is the cartoon version of why this needs to be a designed, bounded primitive rather than ad-hoc agent judgment: define WHICH action classes require sign-off vs proceed freely.
